patch.description: Partial (merge) update of a greenhouse's target bundle — the operator's ad-hoc edit (frontend interactions: edit → confirm → optimistic pending → settled/rolled back). In 2a this is a thin relay to the controller's REST `PATCH /setpoints`; in 2b it becomes sticky intended state under reconciliation (platform §5). Out-of-range values and cross-field invariants (e.g. humidity_low_pct < humidity_high_pct) are rejected with 422 naming the violated bound.

200.description: The target bundle after applying the edit.

post.description: The optimizer's single-authority setpoint submission (RFC-005). Same partial merge as the operator's PATCH edit, but recorded with `source = optimizer` provenance and returned 202 Accepted. Its authentication is the config-gated service-auth boundary (RFC-011): with `SERVICE_AUTH_MODE=trusted_network` (default) it accepts the untokened internal call; with `SERVICE_AUTH_MODE=oidc` it requires a Keycloak client-credentials token carrying the narrow `setpoints:write` role (or an operator token) and rejects unauthenticated calls 401 / non-privileged 403. The `{}` scheme is listed first, so the token is optional at the contract level — enforcement is gated on `SERVICE_AUTH_MODE`.

202.description: The submission was accepted; the returned bundle is the resulting intended state (delivered to the controller, or held when it is offline).
